explanation: This command bypasses the Sigma rule by utilizing two evasion techniques against the rigid string matching. First, it uses the shortened parameter alias '-f' instead of the full '-Filter', which breaks the first 'CommandLine|contains|all' condition. Second, it uses the 'Export-Csv' cmdlet to write the data to a file, which is missing from the list of explicitly monitored output methods (' > ', 'Out-File', 'Set-Content', etc.) in the second 'CommandLine|contains' condition.

This command bypasses the rule because the filter parameter does not use the exact string ' -Filter *' (it filters for enabled users instead, a common alternative to dumping all users). By missing the specific ' -Filter *' string, the 'CommandLine|contains|all' condition evaluates to false, even though the output is being redirected using a caught method ('Out-File').
